title: Why Upstart Stock Is Trading Flat Following Revenue Beat, EPS Miss
text: Upstart Holdings (UPST) is trading flat despite beating revenue expectations because the company missed on profitability metrics, creating a mixed earnings picture that leaves investors cautious. The company delivered revenue of $308.21 million, surpassing the consensus estimate of $303.68 million. However, adjusted EPS came in at 30 cents versus the expected 42 cents—a significant shortfall that overshadowed the top-line beat. Beyond the EPS miss, margin compression is the real story. Adjusted EBITDA margin at 13%, down from 20% in the prior-year quarter, which explains investor hesitation. Operating losses also widened to $7.5 million from $4.5 million year-over-year, despite revenue growing 44%. The stock remains deeply troubled on longer timeframes: down 38.46% over the past 12 months and trading significantly below its key moving averages. MACD momentum indicators are showing weakness. While loan originations surged 77% year-over-year, investors punished the stock for deteriorating profit margins and missed earnings guidance—a classic case where growth isn't translating into bottom-line results.
